Question: Reference to AIP Viet Nam, The Class D airspace are classified and designated in accordance with the following :
AnswerA: The responsibility areas of the TWRs at the civil aerodromes, the aerodromes (using both civil and military) over the territory of Viet Nam (except CTR of Noi Bai , Da Nang, Tan Son Nhat).
AnswerB:
On the ATS routes: From minimum flight level to 6 000 M.
 TMA: Noi Bai, Tan Son Nhat.
 CTR: Noi Bai, Tan Son Nhat, Da Nang
AnswerC:
Unspecified airspace;
 The responsibility areas of the TWRs of which operations are based on time limits or from sunrise to sunset: out of operation time and service provided.
AnswerD:
Above 14 000 M
 Lateral limit: Ha Noi and Ho Chi Minh FIRs.
 From 2 000 M to 6 000 M
 Lateral limit: Ha Noi and Ho Chi Minh FIRs, except ATS routes and TMA, CTR of C and D classes.
CorrectAnswer: The responsibility areas of the TWRs at the civil aerodromes, the aerodromes (using both civil and military) over the territory of Viet Nam (except CTR of Noi Bai , Da Nang, Tan Son Nhat).

Question: Reference to AIP Viet Nam, The Class E airspace are classified and designated in accordance with the following :
AnswerA:
Lateral limit: Ha Noi and Ho Chi Minh FIRs
 Vertical limit: from 6 000 M to 14 000 M
AnswerB:
On the ATS routes: From minimum flight level to 6 000 M.
 TMA: Noi Bai, Tan Son Nhat.
 CTR: Noi Bai, Tan Son Nhat, Da Nang
AnswerC:
Unspecified airspace;
 The responsibility areas of the TWRs of which operations are based on time limits or from sunrise to sunset : out of operation time and service provided.
AnswerD:
Above 14 000 M
 Lateral limit: Ha Noi and Ho Chi Minh FIRs.
 From 2 000 M to 6 000 M
 Lateral limit: Ha Noi and Ho Chi Minh FIRs, except ATS routes and TMA, CTR of C and D classes.
CorrectAnswer:
Unspecified airspace;
 The responsibility areas of the TWRs of which operations are based on time limits or from sunrise to sunset : out of operation time and service provided.

Question: Reference to AIP Viet Nam, The Class G airspace are classified and designated in accordance with the following :
AnswerA:
IFR flights only are permitted, all flights are provided with air traffic control service and are separated from each other.
 Radio communication requirement:
 Continuous two-way
AnswerB:
IFR and VFR flights are permitted, all flights are provided with air traffic control service and IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and from VFR flights. VFR flights are separated from IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of other VFR flights.
 Radio communication requirement:
 Continuous two-way
AnswerC:
IFR and VFR flights are permitted and all flights are provided with air traffic control service, IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of VFR flights, VFR flights receive traffic information in respect of all other flights.
 Radio communication requirement:
 Continuous two-way
AnswerD:
IFR and VFR flights are permitted, IFR flights are provided with air traffic control service and are separated from other IFR flights. All flights receive traffic information as far as is practical. 
 Radio communication requirement :
 Continuous two-way
CorrectAnswer:
IFR and VFR flights are permitted, IFR flights are provided with air traffic control service and are separated from other IFR flights. All flights receive traffic information as far as is practical. 
 Radio communication requirement :
 Continuous two-way

Question: The request to change from IFR flight to VFR flight is:
AnswerB: Suggested by ATC when VMC exist

Question: The speed reductions not exceeding plus/minus 40km/h (20kt) IAS should be used for :
AnswerA: Aircraft on inital and intermediate approach segments.
AnswerC: Aircraft on intermediate and final approach segments.
CorrectAnswer: Aircraft on intermediate and final approach segments.

Question: Radar separation based upon primary radar shall be the distance:
AnswerA: Between the edges of the radar blips
AnswerB: Between the centers of the radar blips.
AnswerC: Between the edges of one blip and the center of the other.
AnswerD: Between the nearest edges of the blips.
CorrectAnswer: Between the centers of the radar blips.

Question: Transfer of identity of a radar blip from the radar controller to another should be attempted wheen it is considered that:
AnswerA: The aircraft is on the frequency of the accepting controller.
AnswerB: The aircraft is over the common boundary.
AnswerC: The aircraft is within the coverage of the accepting controller’s rasdar display .
AnswerD: The aircraft reports over the last fix displayed on the radar display of transferring controller.
CorrectAnswer: The aircraft is within the coverage of the accepting controller’s rasdar display .

Question: For traffic on reciprocal tracks where lateral separation is not provide, vertical separation shall be kept at least…………prior to and after the time the aircraft are estimated to pass, or are estimated to have passed.
AnswerD: As long as the aircraft are estimated to have passed each other.

Question: According to the 125 Decree on Air Traffic Management: The Operation Department (Ministry of Nation Defense) shall designate and air traffic control services, aeronautical information services providers of danger areas at least………hours prior any operations performed that may give risk to civil flight operations.

The generation of minimum safe altitude warnings is a function of an ATC radar-processing system. The objective of the MSAW function is to assist in the prevention of controlled flight into………………accidents by generating in a timely manner, a warning of the possible infringement of a minimum safe altitude.

The criterion which shall be used to determine that a specific level is occupied by an aircraft shall be +_60m(200ft) in RVSM airspace. In other airspace, it shall be………..,except that the appropriate ATS authority may specify a smaller criterion, but not less than +_60m(200ft), if this is found to be more practical.
When SSR radar is not available, radar identification may be achieved by instructing the aircraft to make heading changes of at least:
Vertical separation if other known traffic behind aircraft dumping fuel within 15 minutes flying time or a distance of 93km (50NM) by at least 300m (1000ft) if above the aircraft dumping fuel; and
AnswerA: At least 600m (2000ft) if below the aircraft dumping fuel.
AnswerB: At least 300m (1000ft) if below the aircraft dumping fuel.
AnswerC: At least 900m (3000ft) if below the aircraft dumping fuel.
AnswerD: At least 1200m (4000ft) if below the aircraft dumping fuel.
CorrectAnswer: At least 900m (3000ft) if below the aircraft dumping fuel.

Question: Whenever a code is assigned to an aircraft, the setting of this code shall be verified by controller:
The Director of Airport Authority shall decide to temporarily close an airport or aerodrome for not more than ……….hours and report such decision to the Minister of Transport without delay.
Who have the right to open an airport or aerodrome based on the airport or aerodrome system plans?

Vietnamese and foreign aircraft operating international flights shall be permitted only to take off and land at the international airports. In the case of take off and landing at domestic airports or aerodromes, the shall acquire approval from the:
The establishment and operation of airways shall be approved by:
Who has the right to grant flight permission to foreign VIP flights carrying the guests of the Party and State and escorting flights or other flights associated with the VIP flight.
…………………shall decide to establish the prohibited and restricted area inthe territory of VietNam in order to ensure the national defense, security, and social safety.
If an arriving aircraft is making a straight-in approach, a departing aircraft may take off in any direction until……….. before the arriving aircraft is estimated to be over the instrument runway.
If an arriving aircraft is making a complete instrument approach, a departing aircraft may take off in any direction until:
AnswerA: The arriving aircraft has started its procedure turn or base turn leading to final approach.
AnswerB: The arriving aircraft has commenced initial approach.
AnswerC: The arriving aircraft has entered ILS glide path.
AnswerD: The pilot of the arriving aircraft has observed the departing aircraft.
CorrectAnswer: The arriving aircraft has started its procedure turn or base turn leading to final approach.

Question: Radar separation between two wake turbulence category “ HEAVY” aircrafts on final shall be at least:

When aircraft operating within controlled airspace needs to dump fuel, the flight crew shall advise ATC. The ATC unit should then coordinate with the flight crew the following: the route to be flown, which, if possible, should be clear of cities and towns, preferably over water and away from areas where thunderstorms have been reported or are expected; The duration of the fuel dumping;The level to be used, which should be not less than……….
Other known traffic should be separated from the aircraft dumping fuel by:
AnswerA: At least 19km(10NM) horizontally, but not behind the aircraft dumping fuel .
AnswerB: Vertical separation if behind the aircraft dumping fuel within 10 minutes flying time or a distance of 93km(50NM)….
AnswerC: At least 19km(10NM) horizontally, but not front of the aircraft dumping fuel.
AnswerD: At least 37km(20NM) horizontally, but not behind the aircraft dumping fuel.
CorrectAnswer: At least 19km(10NM) horizontally, but not behind the aircraft dumping fuel .

Question: To determine effect of wake turbulence, aircraft are grouped into the following categories:
AnswerA: CAT I, CAT II, CAT III;
AnswerD: CAT A, CAT B, CAT C.

Question: The controller shall…………. To ascertain that the clearance or instruction has been correctly acknowledge by flight crew and shall take immediately action to correct any discrepancies revealed by the………….
Minimum flight altitude ensures (according to Annex II rules of the air):
AnswerC: At least 300m(1000ft) above highest obstacle in plain area, 600m above highest obstacle in high terrain mountainuos area.
CorrectAnswer: At least 300m(1000ft) above highest obstacle in plain area, 600m above highest obstacle in high terrain mountainuos area.

Question: Which aircraft along the following has first priority:
AnswerD: Arriving aircraft in the traffic pattern.

Question: An aircraft making a radar approach should be advised to consider executing a missed approach if the aircraft is not visible on the radar display for significant interval during the last…….. of the approach.
Speed adjustments may not be requested or applied after the aircraft has passed a point of…………… from the threshold on final approach:
Before providing radar service to an aircraft, the radar controller shall:
AnswerA: Establish radar identity of that aircraft.
AnswerB: Provide the radar information to the aircraft.
AnswerC: Request the pilot to change heading 30 degrees or more.
AnswerD: Instruct the pilot to operate SPI feature.
CorrectAnswer: Establish radar identity of that aircraft.

AnswerA: Line up immediately after the landing aircraft

Question: Surveillance radar:
AnswerA: Radar equipment used to determine the altitude of an aircraft.
AnswerB: Radar equipment used to determine the position of an aircraft in range and azimuth.
AnswerC: Radar equipment used to determine the position of an aircraft in range.
AnswerD: Radar equipment used to determine the position of an aircraft in azimuth.
CorrectAnswer: Radar equipment used to determine the position of an aircraft in range and azimuth.

Question: Mode A code to provide recognition of an aircraft in an emergency:
Mode A code to provide recognition of an aircraft with radio communication failure:
Mode A code to provide recognition of an aircraft which is being subjected to unlawful interference:
Mode A code shall be reserved to provide recognition of an aircraft which has not received any instructions from air traffic control units to operate the transponder.
The Mode A code shall be manually selected from……….
Air traffic service, a generic term meaning variously, shall comprise:
AnswerB: Alerting service, air traffic advisory service.

Question: The order of priority for the transmission of messages in the aeronautical fixed telecommunication network shall be as follows: Transmission priority indicator
The four-letter location indicators are listed in …………
The three-letter designators are listed in………
AFTN destination stations shall retain, for a period of at least……..
Except for reasons of safety, no transmission shall be directed to an aircraft ………
AnswerB: During the last part of the final approach.

Question: Airborne collision avoidance system (ACAS).
AnswerA: An aircraft system based on secondary surveillance radar (SSR) transponder signals.
AnswerB: An aircraft system based on primary radar signals.
CorrectAnswer: An aircraft system based on secondary surveillance radar (SSR) transponder signals.

Question: Pressure-altitude: An atmospheric pressure expressed in terms of …which corresponds to that pressure in the Standard Atmosphere.
Radio navigation service: A service providing …or position data for the efficient and safe operation of aircraft supported by one or more radio navigation aids.
Touchdown is the point where the nominal glide path intercepts..........

ILS reference datum :A point at a specified height located above the intersection of the runway centre line and the threshold and through which …extended straight portion of the ILS glide path passes.
The DME system shall comprise:
AnswerA: one component fitted in the aircraft.
AnswerB: one component installed on the ground.

Question: Air-ground communication:
AnswerA: One-way communication between aircraft and stations or locations on the surface of the earth.
AnswerB: Two-way communication between aircraft and stations or locations on the surface of the earth.
CorrectAnswer: Two-way communication between aircraft and stations or locations on the surface of the earth.

Question: Air-to-ground communication:
AnswerA: One-way communication from aircraft to stations or locations on the surface of the earth.
AnswerB: Two-way communication from aircraft to stations or locations on the surface of the earth.
CorrectAnswer: One-way communication from aircraft to stations or locations on the surface of the earth.

Question: Ground-to-air communication:
AnswerA: One-way communication from stations or locations on the surface of the earth to aircraft.
AnswerB: Two-way communication from stations or locations on the surface of the earth to aircraft.
CorrectAnswer: One-way communication from stations or locations on the surface of the earth to aircraft.

Question: Controller-pilot data link communications (CPDLC).
AnswerA: A means of communication between controllers, using data link for ATC communications.
AnswerB: A means of communication between controller and pilot, using data link for ATC communications.
AnswerC: A means of communication between pilots, using data link for ATC communications.
CorrectAnswer: A means of communication between controller and pilot, using data link for ATC communications.

Question: Air-report:
AnswerA: A report from an aircraft in flight.
AnswerB: A report from an aircraft on ground.
AnswerC: A report from an aircraft in the air.
AnswerD: A report from an aircraft in hangar.
CorrectAnswer: A report from an aircraft in flight.

Question: Distress messages (priority indicator SS).
AnswerA: This message category shall comprise those messages sent by mobile stations reporting that they are threatened by grave and imminent danger .
AnswerB: This message category shall comprise those messages sent by mobile stations reporting that they are in danger.
AnswerC: This message category shall comprise those messages sent by ATC unit reporting that they are in danger.
AnswerD: This message category shall comprise those messages sent by area control centre reporting that they are in danger.
CorrectAnswer: This message category shall comprise those messages sent by mobile stations reporting that they are threatened by grave and imminent danger .

Question: Urgency messages (priority indicator DD).
AnswerA: This category shall comprise messages concerning the safety of a ship, aircraft or other vehicles, or of some person on board or within sight .
AnswerB: This category shall comprise messages concerning the safety of a ship.
AnswerC: This category shall comprise messages concerning the safety of an aircraft.
AnswerD: This category shall comprise messages concerning the safety of some person on board.
CorrectAnswer: This category shall comprise messages concerning the safety of a ship, aircraft or other vehicles, or of some person on board or within sight .

Question: What is a PAPI
PANS-OPS means
AnswerA: Procedures for Air Navigation Systems - Airfield Operations
AnswerB: Procedures for Air Navigation Services - Aircraft Operations
AnswerC: Pilots Alternate Navigational Systems and Operational Procedures
AnswerD: Primary Alternate Navigational Systems and Operational Procedures
CorrectAnswer: Procedures for Air Navigation Services - Aircraft Operations

Question: PANS are
VORTAC is
AnswerA: A combined VOR and TACAN combination where bearing is from the VOR element and the range from TACAN element
AnswerB: A combined VOR and TACAN combination where bearing is from the TACAN and the range from VOR
AnswerC: Range and bearing are supplied from TACAN element and the VOR is a switch on device
AnswerD: TACAN refined for missed approach positioning
CorrectAnswer: A combined VOR and TACAN combination where bearing is from the VOR element and the range from TACAN element

Question: With increasing altitude, the rate of climb will:
AnswerA: decreases because power available increases and power required increases
AnswerB: increases because power available decreases and power required increases
AnswerC: decreases because power available decreases and power required increases
CorrectAnswer: decreases because power available decreases and power required increases

Question: If the aircraft mass increases, how does the rate of climb:
A headwind will:
AnswerA: increase the climb flight path angle
AnswerB: decrease the climb flight path angle
AnswerC: no effect the angle of climb
CorrectAnswer: increase the climb flight path angle

Question: In a standard atmosphere and at the sea level, the calibrated airspeed (CAS) is:
An airspeed indicator displays:
On board an aircraft, the altitude is measured from the:
With regards to the optimum altitude during the cruise, the aircraft is:
AnswerA: flown as close to the optimum altitude as ATC will allow
AnswerB: always flown 2000 ft below the optimum altitude
AnswerC: may be flown above or below the optimum altitude, but never at the optimum altitude
CorrectAnswer: flown as close to the optimum altitude as ATC will allow

When descending below the optimum altitude at the long range cruise speed:

AnswerA: It will increase the take-off distance
AnswerB: It will decrease the take-off distance
AnswerC: It will increase the take-off distance available
CorrectAnswer: It will increase the take-off distance

Question: For a jet transport aeroplane, which of the following is the reason for the use of maximum range speed
An aircraft with 180 minutes ETOPS approval must be:
AnswerA: no more than 180 minutes from a suitable alternate, still air at the one engine inoperative TAS
AnswerB: no more than 60 minutes from a suitable alternate, in the prevailing conditions and using the one engine inoperative TAS
AnswerC: no more than 90 minutes from a suitable alternate, in the prevailing conditions and using the one engine inoperative TAS
CorrectAnswer: no more than 180 minutes from a suitable alternate, still air at the one engine inoperative TAS

Question: All current civil SSR systems use :
AnswerA: The same frequencies i.e. 1030MHz for the ground to air transmission and 1090MHz for the air to ground transmission.
AnswerB: The same frequencies i.e. 1090MHz for the ground to air transmission and 1030MHz for the air to ground transmission.
AnswerC: The same frequency i.e. 1030MHz for transmissions on both directions .
CorrectAnswer: The same frequencies i.e. 1030MHz for the ground to air transmission and 1090MHz for the air to ground transmission.

Question: If an unidentified aircraft is approaching heading on the controlled aircraft what action should be taken by the radar controller?
AnswerA: Immediately turn the controlled aircraft to the right
AnswerB: Descend the controlled aircraft to fly below the unidentified aircraft
AnswerC: When the aircraft is 5 miles apart turn the controlled aircraft to the right
CorrectAnswer: Immediately turn the controlled aircraft to the right

Question: How is Flight Visibility defined?
AnswerA: The visibility reported by a pilot currently flying in the vicinity
AnswerB: The measured visibility assessed by a certified meteorological observer
AnswerC: The visibility forward from the flight deck of an aeroplane in flight
AnswerD: The forecast visibility at 3000 ft above the aerodrome
CorrectAnswer: The visibility forward from the flight deck of an aeroplane in flight

Question: An aircraft is considered to overtake another if it approaches the other aircraft from the rear on a line forming an angle of less than:
AnswerA: 60 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
AnswerB: 50 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
AnswerC: 80 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
AnswerD: 70 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
CorrectAnswer: 70 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter

Question: Pilots should notify air traffic control a revised estimated time if the reported estimate for the reporting point is found to be in error in excess of:

AnswerA: Clear of clouds and sight of surface ; 8km visibility
AnswerB: 2000ft horizontally , 1000ft vertically from clouds ; 5 km visibility
AnswerC: 1500m horizontally , 1000ft vertically from clouds ; 5 km visibility
AnswerD: 1500m horizontally , 1000ft vertically from clouds ;8 km visibility
CorrectAnswer: 1500m horizontally , 1000ft vertically from clouds ; 5 km visibility

Question: The criterion which shall be used to determine that a specific level is occupied by an aircraft shall be ±60m(200ft) in RVSM airspace. AnswerA: At least 600m (2000ft) if below the aircraft dumping fuel.

Red pyrotechnic directed by tower to an aircraft in flight means:
AnswerB: Not withstanding any previous instruction, do not land for the time being
AnswerC: Give way to other aircraft and continue circling
AnswerD: Clearance to and taxi will be given in due course.
CorrectAnswer: Not withstanding any previous instruction, do not land for the time being

Question: In the event of a delay of …..minutes in excess of the estimated off-block time for a controlled flight or delay of …minutes for an uncontrolled flight for which a flight plan has been submitted , the flight plan should be amended or a new flight plan submitted and the old flight plan cancelled, whichever is applicable :

If a pilot who has experienced two-way radio failure during an IFR flight proceeds according to his latest clearance , which was the navigational aid serving his destination airport , where should he commence his descent for landing ?
AnswerA: When he is 80 miles from the airport
AnswerC: When he is over the airport
AnswerD: From the navigational aid serving his destination
CorrectAnswer: From the navigational aid serving his destination

Question: Aircraft taxiing in the manoeuvring area must give way to

An operator must ensure that, for the duration of each flight, be kept on the ground a copy of the :
Visual approach is :
AnswerA: An approach made by an IFR flight when either part or all of an instrument approach procedure is not completed.
AnswerB: An approach made by VFR flight.
AnswerC: Executed in visual reference to terrain.

Question: Clearance limit means :
AnswerA: The point to which an aircraft is granted an air traffic control clearance .
AnswerB: The destination airport at which an aircraft plans to arrive.
AnswerC: The flight level at which an aircraft plans to fly.
AnswerD: To point at which an aircraft is going to hold.
CorrectAnswer: The point to which an aircraft is granted an air traffic control clearance .

Question: Touchdown zone is:
AnswerA: The portion of a runway , beyond the threshold , where it is intended landing airplanes first contact the runway.
AnswerB: The portion of a runway, at the threshold, where it is intended landing airplanes first contact the runway .
AnswerC: The beginning of a runway where it is intended landing airplanes first contact the runway .
AnswerD: The portion of a runway where it is intended landing airplanes first contact the runway .
CorrectAnswer: The portion of a runway , beyond the threshold , where it is intended landing airplanes first contact the runway.

Question: Expected approach time is :
AnswerA: The time at which ATC expects that an arriving aircraft , following a delay will leave the holding point to complete its approach for landing .
AnswerB: The time at which the pilot of an arriving aircraft expects to leave the holding point to complete its approach for landing
AnswerC: The time at which it is estimated that an arriving aircraft will commence its approach for landing
CorrectAnswer: The time at which ATC expects that an arriving aircraft , following a delay will leave the holding point to complete its approach for landing .

Question: Missed approach procedure is :
AnswerA: The procedure to be followed if an aircraft cannot land
AnswerB: The procedure which aircraft uses when there are many obstacles on the Runway-in–use
AnswerC: The procedure to be commenced by aircraft decision height
AnswerD: The procedure to be followed if the approach cannot be continued.
CorrectAnswer: The procedure to be followed if the approach cannot be continued.

Question: Cruising levels at which a flight is to be conducted mean :

Question: Thinking on human reliability is changing:
AnswerA: It is thought that will be possible to eliminate errors in the near future.
AnswerB: Human errors are now considered as being inherent to the cognitive function of human and are generally inescapable .
AnswerC: Human errors can be avoide All it takes is to be vigilant and to extend one’s knowledge.
AnswerD: The individual view of safety has gradually replaced the systemic view of safety.
CorrectAnswer: Human errors are now considered as being inherent to the cognitive function of human and are generally inescapable .

Question: Which of the following statements in regard to motivation is correct?
AnswerA: Too much motivation may result in hypovigilance and thus in a decrease in attention.

AnswerC: Low motivation will guarantee adequate attention management capabilities.
AnswerD: Extremely high motivation in combination with excessive stress will limit attention management capabilities.
CorrectAnswer: Extremely high motivation in combination with excessive stress will limit attention management capabilities.

Question: Once detected, an error will result in cognitive consequences which:

CorrectAnswer: is necessary to maintain good situational awareness

Question: Stress is above all:

Question: Which of the following statements is true about error chain?

CorrectAnswer: Many accidents are a linked sequence where several layers of defences fail .

Question: Which of the following statements is true about chain of event?

Question: What should a radar controller do if the code displayed on the radar screen difference from the code he has just asked the pilot to squawk?
AnswerA: Request the pilot to stop squawk.
AnswerB: Request the pilot to recycle the assigned mode and code.
AnswerC: The pilot shall be requested to confirm the code and reselect the correct code .
AnswerD: Request the pilot to switch the transponder from ON to STANDBY.
CorrectAnswer: The pilot shall be requested to confirm the code and reselect the correct code .

Question: An aircraft will consider to have reached the level to which it has been cleared when:
AnswerA: Level information derived by mode C indicated that it is within 90m ( 300 ft) of it’s assigned level.
AnswerB: After three consecutive renewals of mode C derived level information.
AnswerC: As long as mode C derived level information indicated that it is within plus or minus 90m (300ft).
AnswerD: Three consecutive renewals of mode C derived level information have indicated that it is within 90m(300ft) of it’s assigned levels.
CorrectAnswer: Level information derived by mode C indicated that it is within 90m ( 300 ft) of it’s assigned level.

Question: Radar separation may be applied between an aircraft taking off and other radar controlled traffic provided there is reasonable assurance that:
AnswerA: The taking off a/c will be identified within 1 NM from the end of the runway and at the time the required radar separation will exist.
AnswerB: The taking–off a/c is at or above the minimum flight altitude of minimum safety altitude.
AnswerC: Both taking – off a/c and other radar controller flight are being identified.
CorrectAnswer: The taking off a/c will be identified within 1 NM from the end of the runway and at the time the required radar separation will exist.

Question: In the case that the radar fails, but communication is still good, the radar controller should:
AnswerA: Instruct aircraft to maintain level and VMC.
AnswerB: Inform aircraft that radar fails and maintain own separation.
AnswerC: Instructed aircraft to change to APP frequency.
AnswerD: Mark position of identified aircraft and take measure to provide non- radar separation.
CorrectAnswer: Mark position of identified aircraft and take measure to provide non- radar separation.

Question: An arriving aircraft is descending for approach. The pilot of the aircraft is to change altimeter setting from Standard air pressure (760 mm Pb) to QNH (or QFE) ___
AnswerB: Below transition level but above transition altitude
CorrectAnswer: Below transition level but above transition altitude

Question: What is Standard Instrument Arrival (STAR)?
AnswerA: A designated instrument flight rule (IFR) arrival route linking a significant point, normally on an ATS route, with a point from which a published instrument approach procedure can be commenced.
AnswerB: A designated visual flight rule (IFR) arrival route linking a significant point, normally on an ATS route, with a point from which a published instrument approach procedure can be commenced.
AnswerC: A designated instrument flight rule (IFR) arrival route linking a significant point, normally on an ATS route, with a point from which a published visual approach procedure can be commenced.
CorrectAnswer: A designated instrument flight rule (IFR) arrival route linking a significant point, normally on an ATS route, with a point from which a published instrument approach procedure can be commenced.

Question: The aircraft should be advised to consider executing a missed approach if the aircraft is not visible on the situation display for any significant interval during the last...of the approach.
If the landing clearance is not received, a subsequent notification should be made at approximately…and landing clearance requested.
Which does ATC Term “Radar contact” signify?
AnswerA: Your aircraft has been identified and you will receive separation from all aircraft while in contact with this radar facility.
AnswerB: You will be given traffic advisories until advised that the service has been terminated or that radar contact has been lost.
AnswerC: ATC is receiving your transponder and will furnish vectors and traffic advisories until you are advised that contact has been lost.
AnswerD: Your aircraft has been identified on the radar display and radar flight instructions will provided until radar identification is terminated.
CorrectAnswer: Your aircraft has been identified on the radar display and radar flight instructions will provided until radar identification is terminated.

Question: What does an air traffic controller have to do before providing an ATS surveillance service to an air craft?
AnswerC: Issue ATC clearance for air craft

Question: What does mode C show?
What does mode S show?
How shall a pilot inform a radar control unit that the aircraft is not quipped with transponder?
Track separation between aircraft using the same VOR is at least…and at a distance of…more from the facility.
Track separation between aircraft using the same NDB is at least…and at a distance of…more from the facility.
Track separation between aircraft using dead reckoning is at least…and at a distance of…more from the facility.
What type of separation must be maintained at all times between aircraft holding in the holding pattern?
AnswerD: a combination of lateral and vertical

Question: Priority for landing shall be given to:
AnswerA: an aircraft which anticipates being compelled to land because of factors affecting the safe operation of the aircraft (engine failure, shortage of fuel, etc.);
AnswerB: hospital aircraft or aircraft carrying any sick or seriously injured persons requiring urgent medical attention;
AnswerC: aircraft engaged in search and rescue operations; and other aircraft as may be determined by the appropriate authority.

Question: Speed control shall not be applicable to aircraft:
AnswerA: Entering or established in holding pattern.
AnswerB: Encountering the turbulent weather; conducting priority/ emergency landing.

Question: AIP Amendment contain:
AnswerA: Permanent changes to the information contained in the AIP.
AnswerB: Temporary changes to the information contained in the AIP.
CorrectAnswer: Permanent changes to the information contained in the AIP.

Question: AIP Supplement contain:
AnswerA: Permanent changes to the information contained in the AIP which are published by means of special pages.
AnswerB: Temporary changes to the information contained in the AIP which are published by means of special pages.
CorrectAnswer: Temporary changes to the information contained in the AIP which are published by means of special pages.

Question: Danger area.
AnswerA: An airspace of defined dimensions within which activities dangerous to the flight of aircraft may exist at all times.
AnswerB: An airspace of defined dimensions within which activities dangerous to the flight of aircraft may exist at specified times .
AnswerC: An airspace of defined dimensions within which activities dangerous to the flight of aircraft may exist at day-light times.
AnswerD: An airspace of defined dimensions within which activities dangerous to the flight of aircraft may exist at night times.
CorrectAnswer: An airspace of defined dimensions within which activities dangerous to the flight of aircraft may exist at specified times .

Question: Geodesic distance.
AnswerA: The slant distance between any two points on a mathematically defined ellipsoidal surface.
AnswerB: The distance between any two points on a mathematically defined ellipsoidal surface.
AnswerC: The shortest distance between any two points on a mathematically defined ellipsoidal surface .
AnswerD: The vertical distance between any two points on a mathematically defined ellipsoidal surface.
CorrectAnswer: The shortest distance between any two points on a mathematically defined ellipsoidal surface .

Question: Geodetic datum.
AnswerA: A set of parameters required to define location and orientation of the local reference system with respect to the global reference system/frame.
AnswerB: A maximum set of parameters required to define location and orientation of the local reference system with respect to the global reference system/frame.
AnswerC: A minimum set of parameters required to define location and orientation of the local reference system with respect to the global reference system/frame .
CorrectAnswer: A minimum set of parameters required to define location and orientation of the local reference system with respect to the global reference system/frame .
